management_notes: CEO Victor Cantore has been with the company for years and has shown consistent insider buying at depressed prices (e.g., 30,000 shares purchased at C$1.03 in January 2025 ahead of major catalysts). Cantore reported holdings of ~4.82M shares as of September 2025, demonstrating meaningful skin-in-the-game. Strategic shareholder Eldorado Gold (~27.27% non-diluted as of Dec 2025) provides validation, technical support, and potential long-term M&A optionality - their stake increased from ~9.6% to 27.3% across 2025 in two tranches via private placement and open-market top-up at C$4.00 (Dec 17, 2025), demonstrating institutional conviction. Management has executed a clean discovery-to-feasibility transition: 2025 MRE (May 2025) -> Updated PEA (Sept/Oct 2025) -> Phase 1 Feasibility Study (April 2026) all delivered on schedule. Share-issuance discipline is reasonable - C$34M raised in 2025 at C$1.60-2.27 per share funded directly into FS work, and the December 2025 top-up was at C$4.00 (no dilution, secondary market). 4/5 rather than 5/5 because cumulative issuance has grown the share count meaningfully (now ~141.6M basic vs ~120M historical), and total insider ownership ex-Eldorado is moderate rather than exceptional.
geology_notes: World-class high-grade gold deposit in tier-1 jurisdiction (Abitibi, Quebec). Updated 2025 NI 43-101 MRE (effective May 21, 2025) reports 1.615 Moz Measured & Indicated at 6.14 g/t Au across 8.183 Mt, plus 698 koz Inferred at 4.31 g/t Au across 5.044 Mt. Underground M&I component grades 7.10 g/t Au - exceptional grade in modern Canadian gold context. The 172% increase in M&I tonnage from the 2024 estimate, and a 43% grade improvement, signals true discovery growth rather than category reclassification. April 2026 Feasibility Study converted material to 774 koz of Reserves (Proven 135 koz at 12.12 g/t + Probable 639 koz at 12.10 g/t = 1.989 Mt at 12.10 g/t Au) - the Phase 1 high-grade Champagne Zone is among the highest-grade reserve grades on a modern Canadian project. Multiple zones (Champagne, Denise, Gratien, Grey Cat, Team) provide diversification and exploration upside. Recent intercepts (213.11 g/t over 3.75 m, March 2025) confirm high-grade continuity. Metallurgy supported by toll-mill arrangement in FS - suggests no metallurgical surprises. Score 5/5.
capital_notes: Basic shares outstanding ~141.6M (after Dec 17, 2025 Eldorado top-up to 38.626M shares = 27.27% non-diluted). Cash position ~C$32.6M reported as of Q4 2025. The company has executed considerable equity financings in 2024-2025: ~5.7M units in Oct 2024 PP at C$1.15 (with C$1.40 warrants, 24-month expiry); C$34.2M PP in July-Aug 2025 (11M CFT shares at C$2.27 + 5.798M HD shares at C$1.60); 14.87M shares purchased by Eldorado in Dec 2025 at C$4.00. Stock options: 3.25M granted Nov 21, 2025 at C$2.85 strike, 5-year term (expiry ~Nov 2030); legacy options from prior years estimated at ~5-7M tranches at lower strikes (C$0.50-2.00 range typical for AMX history) but specific outstanding tranches not directly verifiable from press releases without the latest MD&A. RSUs: 2.026M granted Nov 2025 vesting over 3 years. Eldorado holds 207,000 legacy warrants (strike not disclosed in public press releases). With FS now in hand, project capex of C$193.9M Phase 1 plus growth/sustaining will require either project finance, a streaming deal, equity, or a takeout - dilution risk to fund Phase 1 build remains material. 3/5 reflects substantive cash on hand, manageable dilution to date, but remaining capex hurdle and the warrant/option overhang from years of financing.
catalyst_notes: Catalyst pipeline is exceptionally rich and near-term. April 13, 2026 Feasibility Study just delivered (Post-tax NPV C$1.127B at US$3,500/oz, IRR 114.6%, AISC US$910/oz, 0.5-year payback) - this is a near-term game-changer that crystallizes value. Next catalysts: (1) financing decision/strategic partnership announcement (Eldorado at 27% is the obvious takeout candidate); (2) permitting milestones for Phase 1 build targeting first gold 2028; (3) ongoing exploration drilling at Champagne, Denise, Gratien, Team zones for resource expansion and possible Phase 2 expansion case; (4) toll-mill agreement finalization; (5) potential second technical study for Phase 2 (which could materially expand mine life beyond the 5-year Phase 1). Production targeted for 2028 makes this a genuine development-stage story rather than a perpetual explorer. With FS economics this strong at spot gold and a 27%-shareholder major in the wings, news flow density is high. 5/5.
acquisition_notes: Acquisition setup is among the cleanest in the junior gold space: Eldorado Gold owns 27.27% of issued shares and explicitly increased its stake to that level in December 2025 at C$4.00 - well above their original cost basis - signaling strategic intent. Eldorado runs Lamaque in Quebec (same jurisdiction, similar mining culture) and has expressed ambition to grow Quebec footprint. With FS now showing 774 koz reserves at 12.10 g/t and post-tax NPV C$1.127B at US$3,500/oz, P/NAV math is favorable: at ~141.6M shares and C$5/sh implied market cap C$708M vs C$1.127B post-tax NPV = P/NAV ~0.63x. Comparable Canadian high-grade gold acquisitions over trailing 24 months have transacted at 0.7-1.2x P/NAV (e.g., Probe-Newmont/Goliath, Skeena/major interest, Osisko Mining/Gold Fields Windfall at ~0.85x P/NAV). Eldorado-style strategic creep typically precedes either a takeout offer or further consolidation; given their 27%, they have effective veto over rival bids and visibility into operating economics. 4/5 rather than 5/5 because (a) Eldorado may prefer to remain a strategic minority rather than full takeout if Amex builds Phase 1 itself, and (b) any takeout premium is somewhat capped by Eldorado's blocking position vs an open auction.
analyst_summary:
Amex Exploration (TSXV:AMX) is a development-stage Canadian gold story whose risk/reward profile transformed materially over 2025-2026. The company delivered an updated 1.615 Moz Measured & Indicated resource at 6.14 g/t Au at the Perron Project in Quebec's Abitibi belt (effective May 2025), followed by an updated PEA in Q4 2025, and culminated in a positive Phase 1 Feasibility Study released April 13, 2026, showing post-tax NPV of C$1.127B, IRR of 114.6%, AISC of US$910/oz, and a payback of 0.5 years at US$3,500/oz gold. Phase 1 reserves total 774 koz at 12.10 g/t Au - one of the highest reserve grades on any Canadian gold project advancing to construction.

The shareholder register is now anchored by Eldorado Gold, which raised its stake to 27.27% in December 2025 via a C$59.5M open-market purchase at C$4.00/share - a clear strategic statement, since Eldorado already operates the Lamaque mine in Quebec and benefits operationally from Amex's high-grade ore. CEO Victor Cantore and his board have shown disciplined capital deployment (raises matched to specific catalysts) and personal insider buying through the cycle. Resource quality, jurisdictional advantages, FS economics, and a strategic shareholder positioned to either underwrite construction or take the company out collectively support a BUY thesis.

Key risks: Phase 1 capex of C$193.9M will require financing (project debt, streaming, equity, or takeout) and Phase 2 growth capital adds another C$238M; the 27% Eldorado position acts as both a backstop and a ceiling on takeout premiums; cap-table includes a meaningful options/RSU overhang and historical warrants whose specific tranches are not fully detailed in public press releases. Confidence is set to LOW because basic share count is well-grounded in Dec 2025 disclosure but specific warrant/option tranches were estimated from press releases rather than the latest MD&A on SEDAR+ - the share_instruments array reflects only verified tranches.

Composite score 4+5+3+5+4 = 21 -> BUY.

share_instruments.notes: Eldorado Gold legacy warrants (from Feb 2024 strategic investment); strike/expiry not disclosed in public news releases - confirm in latest MD&A
Stock options granted Nov 21, 2025 to directors/officers; 5-year term
RSUs granted Nov 21, 2025; vest in three equal annual tranches; convert 1:1 to shares at vesting
ESTIMATED legacy options outstanding pre-Nov 2025 (typical TSXV junior holds ~3-5% of float in legacy options at varied strikes); placeholder pending MD&A confirmation - confidence flagged 'low'
